title: Log Event Request
description: Request parameters for logging marketing events

allOf.title: AdCP Version Envelope
allOf.description: Release-precision AdCP protocol version negotiation fields. Composed via `allOf` into every AdCP request and response schema so the version semantics live in exactly one place. Distinct from `core/protocol-envelope.json`, which wraps responses at the transport layer (context_id / task_id / status / payload). This envelope is part of the payload itself.

adcp_version.description: Release-precision AdCP version (VERSION.RELEASE, e.g. "3.0", "3.1", "3.1-beta"). On a request: the buyer's release pin — the seller validates against its supported_versions and returns VERSION_UNSUPPORTED on cross-major mismatch, or downshifts to the highest supported release within the same major. On a response: the release the seller actually served — clients SHOULD validate the response against that release's schema, not against their pin. Patches are not negotiated; surface them as build_version on capabilities for operational visibility. When omitted, falls back to adcp_major_version (deprecated) or server default. Buyers SHOULD emit both adcp_version and adcp_major_version through 3.x to remain compatible with sellers that only read the legacy field. NORMALIZATION: SDKs that read full-semver values from bundle metadata (e.g. ComplianceIndex.published_version = "3.1.0-beta.1") MUST normalize to release-precision ("3.1-beta.1") before emitting on the wire — meta-field values are NOT valid wire values.

adcp_major_version.description: DEPRECATED in favor of adcp_version (release-precision string). Servers MUST continue to honor this field through 3.x. Removed in 4.0. Original semantics: the AdCP major version the buyer's payloads conform to. Sellers validate against their supported major_versions and return VERSION_UNSUPPORTED if unsupported. When omitted, the seller assumes its highest supported version.

event_source_id.description: Event source configured on the account via sync_event_sources

test_event_code.description: Test event code for validation without affecting production data. Events with this code appear in the platform's test events UI.

items.title: Event
items.description: A marketing event (conversion, engagement, or custom) for attribution and optimization

event_id.description: Unique identifier for deduplication (scoped to event_type + event_source_id)

enumDescriptions.view_content: User viewed specific content (product, article, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_content: User selected or clicked on content (article, video, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_item: User selected a specific product or item from a list
enumDescriptions.share: User shared content via social or messaging
enumDescriptions.add_to_cart: User added an item to cart
enumDescriptions.remove_from_cart: User removed an item from cart
enumDescriptions.add_to_wishlist: User added an item to a wishlist
enumDescriptions.refund: A purchase was fully or partially refunded (adjusts ROAS)
enumDescriptions.lead: User expressed interest (form submission, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.qualify_lead: Lead qualified by sales or scoring criteria
enumDescriptions.close_convert_lead: Lead converted to a customer or closed deal
enumDescriptions.disqualify_lead: Lead disqualified or marked as not viable
enumDescriptions.subscribe: User subscribed to a service or newsletter
enumDescriptions.schedule: User scheduled an appointment or event
enumDescriptions.submit_application: User submitted an application (loan, job, etc.)

event_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event occurred

uids.description: Universal ID values for user matching

enumDescriptions.rampid: LiveRamp RampID maintained form (XY, 32 bytes on the wire)
enumDescriptions.rampid_derived: LiveRamp RampID derived form (Xi, 48 bytes on the wire)
enumDescriptions.pairid: Publisher Addressable Identity via IAB Tech Lab PAIR protocol
enumDescriptions.hashed_email: SHA-256 hashed email address. Pseudonymous PII, not anonymous — unsalted SHA-256 of the email namespace is recoverable via precomputed dictionaries; treat as PII. See docs/reference/privacy-considerations#unsalted-hashed-identifiers-are-pseudonymous-not-anonymous.
enumDescriptions.publisher_first_party: Publisher's own first-party identifier, opaque to buyers
enumDescriptions.other: Other universal ID type (specify in ext)

hashed_email.description: SHA-256 hash of lowercase, trimmed email address. Buyer must normalize before hashing: lowercase, trim whitespace. Pseudonymous PII, not anonymous — the email namespace is small enough that an unsalted SHA-256 is recoverable via precomputed dictionaries. Treat as PII for retention, consent, and access-control purposes. See docs/reference/privacy-considerations#unsalted-hashed-identifiers-are-pseudonymous-not-anonymous.

hashed_phone.description: SHA-256 hash of E.164-formatted phone number (e.g. +12065551234). Buyer must normalize to E.164 before hashing. Pseudonymous PII, not anonymous — the E.164 namespace is small enough that an unsalted SHA-256 is recoverable via precomputed dictionaries. Treat as PII for retention, consent, and access-control purposes. See docs/reference/privacy-considerations#unsalted-hashed-identifiers-are-pseudonymous-not-anonymous.

click_id.description: Platform click identifier (fbclid, gclid, ttclid, ScCid, etc.)

click_id_type.description: Type of click identifier (e.g. fbclid, gclid, ttclid, msclkid, ScCid)

client_ip.description: Client IP address for probabilistic matching

client_user_agent.description: Client user agent string for probabilistic matching

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

value.description: Monetary value of the event (should be accompanied by currency)

content_ids.description: Item identifiers for catalog attribution. Values are matched against catalog items using the identifier type declared by the catalog's content_id_type field (e.g., SKUs, GTINs, or vertical-specific IDs like job_id).

content_type.description: Category of content associated with the event (e.g., 'product', 'job', 'hotel'). Corresponds to the catalog type when used for catalog attribution.

content_name.description: Name of the product or content

content_category.description: Category of the product or content

num_items.description: Number of items in the event

price.description: Price per unit of this item

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

enumDescriptions.app: Event occurred in a mobile or desktop app
enumDescriptions.phone_call: Event originated from a phone call
enumDescriptions.chat: Event originated from a chat conversation
enumDescriptions.email: Event originated from an email interaction
enumDescriptions.in_store: Event occurred at a physical retail location
enumDescriptions.system_generated: Event generated by an automated system

event_source_url.description: URL where the event occurred (required when action_source is 'website')

custom_event_name.description: Name for custom events (used when event_type is 'custom')

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

idempotency_key.description: Client-generated unique key for this request. Prevents duplicate event logging on retries. MUST be unique per (seller, request) pair to prevent cross-seller correlation. Use a fresh UUID v4 for each request.

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
